reasoning: The Iranian internet has been subjected to severe restrictions, including an active whitelist system, since protests broke out in early 2026. Recent reports up through April 2026 indicate the country is now involved in an active war (the "2026 Iran war") and facing significant strikes. Internet monitoring by NetBlocks confirms that "most ordinary users still face heavy filtering" and traffic is restricted to pre-approved sites on a whitelist. Iranian officials have actively stated that unrestricted access will not be restored, signaling a permanent move toward a state intranet. Given there are only 18 days left until the resolution date of May 1, 2026, the likelihood of these severe restrictions being lifted for a continuous 24-hour period is exceptionally low, essentially requiring a sudden regime collapse or total technical failure of the censorship apparatus. I estimate this at 2%.
